title: Proposing a balanced budget amendment to the Constitution of the United States.
plain_summary: This bill wants to add a new rule to the Constitution that says the government cannot spend more money than it takes in each year. It would also stop the government from borrowing more money by raising the debt limit. If Congress wants to raise taxes, two-thirds of both the House and Senate would have to vote yes. To make this change to the Constitution, two-thirds of Congress and three-fourths of all state governments would need to agree.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Joint Resolution Proposing a Balanced Budget Amendment to the Constitution of the United States
sections.text: This joint resolution proposes adding a new article to the United States Constitution. For this amendment to become part of the Constitution, two-thirds of both the House of Representatives and the Senate must approve it. Then three-fourths of all state legislatures must also approve it. The proposed amendment has three main parts. Section 1 says that in any fiscal year (the government's budget year), the total amount of money the government spends cannot be more than the total amount of money it receives. Section 2 says that the limit on how much money the United States government can borrow from the public cannot be increased. Section 3 says that any bill that would raise taxes or increase government revenue cannot become law unless two-thirds of all members in both the House and Senate vote for it in a roll call vote (where each member's vote is recorded by name).
